hybrid_figure_production

Anita Wray

2026-05-25

setwd("~/Desktop/VermilionRF/VMSURF Species ID/")

in_file <- "~/Desktop/VermilionRF/VMSURF Species ID/metadata/H&L-WCGBTS Combined_Vermilion RF Finclip Metadata_SK_040125_AW.xlsx"

#Now lets load in metadata
passed_metadata <- read_xlsx(in_file) 

table(passed_metadata$`Rubias Species Call`)
## 
##  bocaccio    canary      rebs    sunset vermilion 
##         4       772         1     16068     10150
length(!is.na(passed_metadata$`Rubias Species Call`))
## [1] 36029
meta <- passed_metadata[(passed_metadata$`Rubias Species Call` != 'canary' | is.na(passed_metadata$`Rubias Species Call`)),]
meta <- meta[(meta$`Rubias Species Call` != 'bocaccio' | is.na(meta$`Rubias Species Call`)),]
meta <- meta[(meta$`Rubias Species Call` != 'rebs' | is.na(meta$`Rubias Species Call`)),]

#Make sure depth is a continuous variable
meta$Depth..m. <- as.numeric(meta$Depth..m.)
meta$Fork.length..cm. <- as.numeric(meta$Fork.length..cm.) 

#Convert hybrid samples to hybrid ID
hybrids <- read.csv("~/Desktop/VermilionRF/VMSURF Species ID/newhybrids/ver_sun_w_hybrids/PofZ_results_5_12_25.csv") %>%
  subset(hybridclass != 'PV') %>%
  subset(hybridclass != 'PS') %>%
  select(c('individuals', 'hybridclass'))

hybrid_id <- hybrids$individuals

hybrid_id <- gsub("_", "-", hybrid_id)


meta$`Rubias Species Call`[meta$Specimen.Num %in% hybrid_id] <- 'hybrid'
meta$`Rubias Pop Call`[meta$Specimen.Num %in% hybrid_id] <- 'hybrid'


table(meta$`Rubias Species Call`)
## 
##    hybrid    sunset vermilion 
##        63     16068     10150
meta <- meta %>%
  drop_na(`Rubias Species Call`) %>%
  subset(`Rubias Species Call` != 'rebs')
world <- ne_countries(scale = "large", returnclass = "sf")
path.eez.usa <- ("~/Desktop/VermilionRF/VMSURF Species ID/metadata/shapefiles/EEZ_land_union_v3_202003/")
fnam.eez.usa <- "EEZ_Land_v3_202030.shp"
eez.usa <- st_read(dsn = path.eez.usa, layer = file_path_sans_ext(fnam.eez.usa))
## Reading layer `EEZ_Land_v3_202030' from data source 
##   `/Users/anita.wray/Desktop/VermilionRF/VMSURF Species ID/metadata/shapefiles/EEZ_land_union_v3_202003' 
##   using driver `ESRI Shapefile'
## Simple feature collection with 323 features and 30 fields
## Geometry type: MULTIPOLYGON
## Dimension:     XY
## Bounding box:  xmin: -180 ymin: -90 xmax: 180 ymax: 86.99401
## Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84
states_map <- sf::st_as_sf(maps::map("state", plot = FALSE, fill = TRUE))

dat.eez.usa1 <- fortify(eez.usa) # a 180400x22 dataframe
labels <- read.csv(file = "~/Desktop/VermilionRF/VMSURF Species ID/metadata/paper2_labels.csv")
map_labels <- read.csv(file = "~/Desktop/VermilionRF/VMSURF Species ID/metadata/paper2_labels_2.csv")

map <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = dat.eez.usa1, size = 0.6, color = "grey", fill = 'white',inherit.aes = FALSE)+
  geom_sf(data = world, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_sf(data = states_map, fill = NA, color = "grey50", inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_point(data = meta, aes(x = `LonDD.v2`, 
                                         y = `LatDD`, 
                                         col = factor(`Rubias Species Call`, 
                                                      levels = c('sunset','hybrid','vermilion'))),
             size = 0.5)+
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-113, -128), ylim = c(29, 50.5), expand = FALSE)+
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(-126, -122, -118)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = color_pal_hybrids)+
  theme(
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.x = element_blank(),
    legend.position = c(0.915, 0.87),
    legend.title=element_blank(),
    legend.spacing = unit(0, "pt"),
    axis.title.x=element_blank(),
    axis.title.y=element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black", fill=NA),
    legend.background = element_blank(),
    legend.box.background = element_rect(colour = "black"))+
  facet_wrap(~factor(`Rubias Species Call`, levels = c('sunset','hybrid','vermilion'))) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 2)))+
  geom_text(data = labels, aes(label = label, x = long, y = lat), size.unit = 'mm', size = 2)+
  geom_segment(data = labels, 
               aes(x = start.arrow.long, y = start.arrow.lat, xend = end.arrow.long, yend = end.arrow.lat), 
               color = 'black', size = 0.5, alpha = 0.6, arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.15, "cm"))) +
  geom_text(data = map_labels, aes(label = label, x= long, y = lat, color = color, angle = angle), size.unit = 'mm', size = 2)

#ggplotly(map + theme(legend.position = 'none'))


ggsave(file = "~/Desktop/VermilionRF/VMSURF Species ID/figures/pop_struct_MS/Fig3-HybridMap.jpeg",
      width = 174,
       height = 110,
       units = c("mm"),
       dpi = 900)
map

dev.off()
## null device 
##           1
vermilion_sites <- unique((meta %>% subset(`Rubias Species Call` == 'vermilion'))$Site.Cell.ID)
sunset_sites <- unique((meta %>% subset(`Rubias Species Call` == 'sunset'))$Site.Cell.ID)
hyb_sites <- unique((meta %>% subset(`Rubias Species Call` == 'hybrid'))$Site.Cell.ID)


(hyb_sites_no_verm <- hyb_sites[which(!hyb_sites %in% vermilion_sites)])
##  [1] "43"  "35"  "137" "569" "289" "587" "531" "525" "596" "518" "549" "249"
## [13] "561" "609"
df <- as.data.frame.matrix(table(meta$Site.Cell.ID, meta$`Rubias Species Call`, useNA = "ifany")) %>%
  subset(hybrid > 0 & vermilion == 0) 


#Change column names
names(meta)[names(meta) == 'Rubias Species Call'] <- 'repunit'
names(meta)[names(meta) == 'Rubias Pop Call'] <- 'collection'

#Create a column which groups the samples into three size classes
#Based on email convo with John Harms
sunverm_hybrid <- meta %>% 
  mutate(sizeclass = case_when(
    Fork.length..cm. >= 35 ~ 'Adult',
    Fork.length..cm. < 35 ~ 'Subadult')
  )

sunverm_hybrid <- sunverm_hybrid %>%
  mutate(collection_method = case_when(
    Survey == 'CB-ESA' ~ 'H&L',
    Survey == 'H&L' ~ 'H&L',
    Survey == 'Hyde Specimen' ~ 'PortSample',
    Survey == 'ODFW-Brookings' ~ 'PortSample',
    Survey == 'WDFW' ~ 'PortSample',
    Survey == 'WCGBTS' ~ 'Trawl'))

#Remove data absent samples
sunverm_hybrid_size <- sunverm_hybrid[!is.na(sunverm_hybrid$sizeclass),]

#Create a age class column
sunverm_hybrid <- sunverm_hybrid %>% 
  mutate(ageclass = case_when(
    Ages <= 5 ~ 'Under 5',
    Ages >5 ~ 'Over 5')
  )
#Remove data absent individuals
sunverm_hybrid_age <- sunverm_hybrid[!is.na(sunverm_hybrid$ageclass),]
library(multcomp)
## Loading required package: mvtnorm
## Loading required package: survival
## Loading required package: TH.data
## Loading required package: MASS
## 
## Attaching package: 'MASS'
## The following object is masked from 'package:plotly':
## 
##     select
## The following object is masked from 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     select
## The following object is masked from 'package:patchwork':
## 
##     area
## 
## Attaching package: 'TH.data'
## The following object is masked from 'package:MASS':
## 
##     geyser
#weight
m1 <- lmer(data = sunverm_hybrid,
           `Wt..kg.` ~ repunit + (1 | Year) + (1 | collection_method))
AIC(m1)
## [1] 57228.27
m2 <- lmer(data = sunverm_hybrid,
           `Wt..kg.` ~ repunit  + (1 | collection_method))
AIC(m2)
## [1] 57444.29
m3 <- lmer(data = sunverm_hybrid,
           `Wt..kg.` ~ repunit  + (1 | Year))
AIC(m3)
## [1] 58233.38
summary(m1)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerMod']
## Formula: Wt..kg. ~ repunit + (1 | Year) + (1 | collection_method)
##    Data: sunverm_hybrid
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 57216.3
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -2.2452 -0.6804 -0.0374  0.5939  6.0103 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups            Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  Year              (Intercept) 0.008356 0.09141 
##  collection_method (Intercept) 0.469340 0.68508 
##  Residual                      0.537697 0.73328 
## Number of obs: 25766, groups:  Year, 19; collection_method, 3
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                  Estimate Std. Error t value
## (Intercept)       1.45703    0.40768   3.574
## repunitsunset     0.14434    0.09273   1.557
## repunitvermilion -0.07530    0.09280  -0.811
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) rpntsn
## repunitsnst -0.226       
## repuntvrmln -0.227  0.994
Anova(m1)
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II Wald chisquare tests)
## 
## Response: Wt..kg.
##          Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## repunit 497.13  2  < 2.2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
ctm_obs.emm <- emmeans(m1, pairwise ~ repunit, adjust = "tukey")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 25766' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 25766)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 25766' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 25766)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
ctm_obs.emm
## $emmeans
##  repunit   emmean    SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  hybrid      1.46 0.408 Inf     0.658      2.26
##  sunset      1.60 0.397 Inf     0.823      2.38
##  vermilion   1.38 0.397 Inf     0.603      2.16
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast           estimate      SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  hybrid - sunset     -0.1443 0.09270 Inf  -1.557  0.2647
##  hybrid - vermilion   0.0753 0.09280 Inf   0.811  0.6960
##  sunset - vermilion   0.2196 0.00985 Inf  22.290  <.0001
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 3 estimates
cld <- cld(ctm_obs.emm, alpha = 0.05, Letters = LETTERS)

# table with factors and 3rd quantile
wt_tbl <- group_by(sunverm_hybrid, repunit) %>%
  summarise(depth=mean(`Wt..kg.`, na.rm = T), sd = sd(`Wt..kg.`, na.rm = T))
# extracting the compact letter display and adding to the Tk table
wt_tbl <- merge(wt_tbl, cld)




# length
m1 <- lmer(data = sunverm_hybrid,
           `Fork.length..cm.` ~ repunit + (1 | Year) + (1 | collection_method))
AIC(m1)
## [1] 179892.3
m2 <- lmer(data = sunverm_hybrid,
           `Fork.length..cm.` ~ repunit  + (1 | collection_method))
AIC(m2)
## [1] 180190.4
m3 <- lmer(data = sunverm_hybrid,
           `Fork.length..cm.` ~ repunit  + (1 | Year))
AIC(m3)
## [1] 183808.2
summary(m1)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerMod']
## Formula: Fork.length..cm. ~ repunit + (1 | Year) + (1 | collection_method)
##    Data: sunverm_hybrid
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 179880.3
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -3.5532 -0.5098  0.1242  0.6567  3.4696 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups            Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  Year              (Intercept)  1.025   1.012   
##  collection_method (Intercept) 63.660   7.979   
##  Residual                      62.189   7.886   
## Number of obs: 25805, groups:  Year, 19; collection_method, 3
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                  Estimate Std. Error t value
## (Intercept)       40.1051     4.7249   8.488
## repunitsunset      1.0508     0.9972   1.054
## repunitvermilion  -0.4980     0.9981  -0.499
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) rpntsn
## repunitsnst -0.210       
## repuntvrmln -0.210  0.994
Anova(m1)
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II Wald chisquare tests)
## 
## Response: Fork.length..cm.
##          Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## repunit 213.64  2  < 2.2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
ctm_obs.emm <- emmeans(m1, pairwise ~ repunit, adjust = "tukey")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 25805' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 25805)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 25805' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 25805)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
ctm_obs.emm
## $emmeans
##  repunit   emmean   SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  hybrid      40.1 4.72 Inf      30.8      49.4
##  sunset      41.2 4.62 Inf      32.1      50.2
##  vermilion   39.6 4.62 Inf      30.6      48.7
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast           estimate    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  hybrid - sunset      -1.051 0.997 Inf  -1.054  0.5429
##  hybrid - vermilion    0.498 0.998 Inf   0.499  0.8718
##  sunset - vermilion    1.549 0.106 Inf  14.611  <.0001
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 3 estimates
cld <- cld(ctm_obs.emm, alpha = 0.05, Letters = LETTERS)

# table with factors and 3rd quantile
length_tbl <- group_by(sunverm_hybrid, repunit) %>%
  summarise(depth=mean(`Fork.length..cm.`, na.rm = T), sd = sd(`Fork.length..cm.`, na.rm = T))
# extracting the compact letter display and adding to the Tk table
length_tbl <- merge(length_tbl, cld)


# depth

m1 <- lmer(data = sunverm_hybrid,
           `Depth..m.` ~ repunit + (1 | Year) + (1 | collection_method))
AIC(m1)
## [1] 245195.3
m2 <- lmer(data = sunverm_hybrid,
           `Depth..m.` ~ repunit  + (1 | collection_method))
AIC(m2)
## [1] 245343.2
m3 <- lmer(data = sunverm_hybrid,
           `Depth..m.` ~ repunit  + (1 | Year))
AIC(m3)
## [1] 246099.9
summary(m1)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerMod']
## Formula: Depth..m. ~ repunit + (1 | Year) + (1 | collection_method)
##    Data: sunverm_hybrid
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 245183.3
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -3.2389 -0.6102 -0.0675  0.6261  7.0367 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups            Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  Year              (Intercept)    8.342  2.888  
##  collection_method (Intercept) 1406.045 37.497  
##  Residual                       783.055 27.983  
## Number of obs: 25800, groups:  Year, 19; collection_method, 3
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                  Estimate Std. Error t value
## (Intercept)        91.974     21.973   4.186
## repunitsunset       8.681      3.538   2.453
## repunitvermilion  -23.369      3.541  -6.599
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) rpntsn
## repunitsnst -0.160       
## repuntvrmln -0.161  0.994
Anova(m1)
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II Wald chisquare tests)
## 
## Response: Depth..m.
##          Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## repunit 7287.7  2  < 2.2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
ctm_obs.emm <- emmeans(m1, pairwise ~ repunit, adjust = "tukey")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 25800' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 25800)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 25800' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 25800)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
ctm_obs.emm
## $emmeans
##  repunit   emmean   SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  hybrid      92.0 22.0 Inf      48.9       135
##  sunset     100.7 21.7 Inf      58.1       143
##  vermilion   68.6 21.7 Inf      26.1       111
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast           estimate    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  hybrid - sunset       -8.68 3.540 Inf  -2.453  0.0377
##  hybrid - vermilion    23.37 3.540 Inf   6.599  <.0001
##  sunset - vermilion    32.05 0.375 Inf  85.356  <.0001
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 3 estimates
cld <- cld(ctm_obs.emm, alpha = 0.05, Letters = LETTERS)

# table with factors and 3rd quantile
depth_tbl <- group_by(sunverm_hybrid, repunit) %>%
  summarise(depth=mean(`Depth..m.`, na.rm = T), sd = sd(`Depth..m.`, na.rm = T))
# extracting the compact letter display and adding to the Tk table
depth_tbl <- merge(depth_tbl, cld)
#Two-way anova of adult and subadult species depth differences
adult <- sunverm_hybrid_size[sunverm_hybrid_size$sizeclass == 'Adult',]
subadult <- sunverm_hybrid_size[sunverm_hybrid_size$sizeclass == 'Subadult',]

m1 <- lmer(data = adult,
           `Depth..m.` ~ repunit  + (1 | Year)+ (1 | collection_method))
AIC(m1)
## [1] 202787.7
summary(m1)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerMod']
## Formula: Depth..m. ~ repunit + (1 | Year) + (1 | collection_method)
##    Data: adult
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 202775.7
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -3.4269 -0.5826 -0.1087  0.6557  6.7448 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups            Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  Year              (Intercept)   12.22   3.495  
##  collection_method (Intercept) 1990.35  44.613  
##  Residual                       641.28  25.323  
## Number of obs: 21794, groups:  Year, 19; collection_method, 3
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                  Estimate Std. Error t value
## (Intercept)       104.333     26.026   4.009
## repunitsunset       9.214      3.461   2.662
## repunitvermilion  -27.200      3.463  -7.854
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) rpntsn
## repunitsnst -0.132       
## repuntvrmln -0.133  0.994
Anova(m1)
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II Wald chisquare tests)
## 
## Response: Depth..m.
##         Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## repunit  9774  2  < 2.2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
ctm_obs.emm <- emmeans(m1, pairwise ~ repunit, adjust = "tukey")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 21794' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 21794)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 21794' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 21794)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
ctm_obs.emm
## $emmeans
##  repunit   emmean   SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  hybrid     104.3 26.0 Inf      53.3       155
##  sunset     113.5 25.8 Inf      63.0       164
##  vermilion   77.1 25.8 Inf      26.6       128
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast           estimate    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  hybrid - sunset       -9.21 3.460 Inf  -2.662  0.0212
##  hybrid - vermilion    27.20 3.460 Inf   7.854  <.0001
##  sunset - vermilion    36.41 0.368 Inf  98.843  <.0001
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 3 estimates
cld <- cld(ctm_obs.emm, alpha = 0.05, Letters = LETTERS)

large_depth_tbl <- cld
large_depth_tbl$sizeclass <- 'Adult'

adult_depth_tbl <- group_by(adult, repunit) %>%
  summarise(depth=mean(`Depth..m.`, na.rm = T), se = (sd(`Depth..m.`, na.rm = T)/sqrt(length(na.omit(`Depth..m.`)))))

juv_depth_tbl <- group_by(subadult, repunit) %>%
  summarise(depth=mean(`Depth..m.`, na.rm = T), se = (sd(`Depth..m.`, na.rm = T)/sqrt(length(na.omit(`Depth..m.`)))))


## Subadults

m1 <- lmer(data = subadult,
           `Depth..m.` ~ repunit  + (1 | Year)+ (1 | collection_method))
AIC(m1)
## [1] 33396.78
summary(m1)
## Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerMod']
## Formula: Depth..m. ~ repunit + (1 | Year) + (1 | collection_method)
##    Data: subadult
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 33384.8
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -3.0869 -0.6244 -0.0382  0.4647  5.3951 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups            Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  Year              (Intercept)    6.964  2.639  
##  collection_method (Intercept) 1038.215 32.221  
##  Residual                       282.647 16.812  
## Number of obs: 3932, groups:  Year, 19; collection_method, 3
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                  Estimate Std. Error t value
## (Intercept)        61.345     19.812   3.096
## repunitsunset       8.582      5.623   1.526
## repunitvermilion   -2.862      5.633  -0.508
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) rpntsn
## repunitsnst -0.282       
## repuntvrmln -0.283  0.994
Anova(m1)
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II Wald chisquare tests)
## 
## Response: Depth..m.
##          Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## repunit 353.05  2  < 2.2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
ctm_obs.emm <- emmeans(m1, pairwise ~ repunit, adjust = "tukey")
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'pbkrtest.limit = 3932' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(pbkrtest.limit = 3932)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
## Note: D.f. calculations have been disabled because the number of observations exceeds 3000.
## To enable adjustments, add the argument 'lmerTest.limit = 3932' (or larger)
## [or, globally, 'set emm_options(lmerTest.limit = 3932)' or larger];
## but be warned that this may result in large computation time and memory use.
ctm_obs.emm
## $emmeans
##  repunit   emmean   SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  hybrid      61.3 19.8 Inf      22.5     100.2
##  sunset      69.9 19.0 Inf      32.7     107.2
##  vermilion   58.5 19.0 Inf      21.2      95.7
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast           estimate   SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  hybrid - sunset       -8.58 5.62 Inf  -1.526  0.2785
##  hybrid - vermilion     2.86 5.63 Inf   0.508  0.8674
##  sunset - vermilion    11.44 0.61 Inf  18.771  <.0001
## 
## Degrees-of-freedom method: asymptotic 
## P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 3 estimates
cld <- cld(ctm_obs.emm, alpha = 0.05, Letters = LETTERS)

small_depth_tbl <- cld
small_depth_tbl$sizeclass <- 'Subadult'


size_depth_tbl <- rbind(large_depth_tbl, small_depth_tbl)



df2 <- as.data.frame.matrix(table(sunverm_hybrid_size$Site.Cell.ID, sunverm_hybrid_size$repunit, useNA = "ifany"))

sunverm_hybrid_adult <- subset(sunverm_hybrid_size, sunverm_hybrid_size$sizeclass == 'Adult')
depth <- ggplot(data = meta,
       aes(x = factor(repunit, levels = c('sunset','hybrid','vermilion')), 
           y = `Depth..m.`,
           col = repunit)) +
  geom_boxplot()+
  theme(legend.position = 'none') +
  #facet_wrap(~sizeclass) + 
  ylab('Depth (m)')+
  xlab('Species')+
  scale_color_manual(values = color_pal_hybrids)+
  ylim(0,350)

(depth_and_size <- ggplot(data = sunverm_hybrid_size,
       aes(x = factor(repunit, levels = c('sunset','hybrid','vermilion')), 
           y = `Depth..m.`,
           col = repunit)) +
  geom_boxplot()+
  geom_text(data = size_depth_tbl, aes(x = factor(repunit, levels = c('sunset','hybrid','vermilion')),
                                y = c(200, 227, 332, 150, 122, 215), label = .group),
            size = 4, color = "black") +
  theme(legend.position = 'none',
        axis.title.x = element_blank()) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0), limits = c(0,350), breaks = c(100,200,300)) +
  facet_wrap(~sizeclass) + 
  ylab('Depth (m)')+
  xlab('Species')+
  scale_color_manual(values = color_pal_hybrids))

(length <- ggplot(data = meta,
       aes(x = factor(repunit, levels = c('sunset','hybrid','vermilion')),
           y = `Fork.length..cm.`,
           col = repunit)) +
  geom_boxplot()+
  theme(legend.position = 'none',
        axis.title.x = element_blank()) +
  ylab('Fork Length (cm)')+
  xlab('Species')+
  scale_color_manual(values = color_pal_hybrids) +
  geom_text(data = length_tbl, aes(x = repunit, y = c(64, 70, 67), label = .group), 
            size = 4, color = "black"))

(weight <- ggplot(data = meta,
       aes(x = factor(repunit, levels = c('sunset','hybrid','vermilion')),
           y = `Wt..kg.`,
           col = repunit)) +
  geom_boxplot()+
  theme(legend.position = 'none',
        axis.title.x = element_blank()) +
  ylab('Weight (kg)')+
  scale_color_manual(values = color_pal_hybrids)+
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0), limits = c(0,6.7), breaks = c(2,4,6)) +
  geom_text(data = wt_tbl, aes(x = repunit,
                               y = c(3.9,6.4,4.5), label = .group), 
            size = 4, color = "black"))

design <- "
BB
DE
"

hybrid_biological <- depth_and_size + length + weight + plot_layout(design = design) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = 'a')


ggsave(file = "~/Desktop/VermilionRF/VMSURF Species ID/figures/pop_struct_MS/SuplFig1-HybridBiological.jpeg",
       width = 180,
       height = 150,
       units = c("mm"),
       dpi = 900)
hybrid_biological

dev.off()
